nodes.description: Surgical removal of shoot apex releasing axillary buds from apical dominance
Shade signal indicating neighbor competition; degrades phyB and FHY3, induces dormancy
Soil nitrogen status; high N upregulates IPT-mediated cytokinin biosynthesis and represses SL
Day length signal acting through CO-FT pathway
Primary carbon source and signaling metabolite; suppresses SL biosynthesis and BRC1, promotes T6P
Promotes DELLA degradation via GID1-SCF-SLY1 pathway
Age-regulated microRNA silencing SPL9 mRNA in juvenile tissues
Constitutive F-box protein in SCF E3 ligase; co-inhibits SMXL678 with D14 (Perception Gate); independently degrades BES1
Short-chain alcohol dehydrogenase catalyzing xanthoxin to abscisic aldehyde; constitutively expressed
Iron-containing carotenoid isomerase catalyzing the first committed step of strigolactone biosynthesis (added iter 2 per Judge S003)
Cytochrome P450 oxidizing carlactone to carlactonoic acid in SL biosynthesis (added iter 2 per Judge S001)
2-OGD-dependent oxidoreductase catalyzing the final known enzymatic step in SL biosynthesis (added iter 2 per Judge S002)
Recruits TPL/TPR co-repressors to BRC1 protein, post-transcriptionally inhibiting BRC1 transactivation (added iter 2 per Judge S005)
Indole-3-acetic acid; near-source hormone (only Decapitation as inhibitor) maintaining apical dominance and promoting SL biosynthesis
Carotenoid cleavage enzyme in strigolactone biosynthesis; max3 mutants overbranch
Carotenoid cleavage enzyme in strigolactone biosynthesis; max4 mutants overbranch
Branching-inhibiting terpenoid lactone hormone; per Perception Gate has only D14 as primary outgoing edge; SL->CKX9 included with Trap 5 disclosure (iter 2)
Alpha/beta hydrolase SL receptor; co-inhibitor of SMXL678 with MAX2; self-degradation after SL perception (Motif 6)
Redundant SMAX1-LIKE transcriptional co-repressors degraded by D14-MAX2 SCF; central hub repressing BRC1 and SPL9, promoting PIN1
Brassinosteroid signaling TF targeted by MAX2-SCF (Motif 5 multi-output); represses BRC1 cooperatively with SMXL678
GRAS transcriptional repressors degraded by GA-GID1-SCF; sequester SPL9 from BRC1 promoter
SBP-domain TF activating BRC1; repressed by miR156, DELLA, SMXL678, FHY3 (no activators - all-inhibitor node)
TCP transcription factor; master integrator with 3 activators (Auxin, SPL9, Low_R_FR) and 7 inhibitors (SMXL678, Cytokinin, BES1, FT, Sucrose, TIE1, Decapitation)
Rate-limiting cytokinin biosynthesis enzyme; activated by nitrogen and decapitation, repressed by auxin

Cytokinin-degrading enzyme; induced by SL signaling (added iter 2 per Judge S004 option a, with Trap 5 framework limitation: signaling-mutant rescue tests for CKX9 may mispredict because SL->CKX9 is a non-gated direct edge violating strict Perception Gate)
Bud outgrowth-promoting hormone; antagonizes SL/auxin via BRC1 repression and PIN1/PIN3 polarization; balanced by IPT (synthesis) and CKX9 (degradation - Motif 4)
Auxin efflux carrier; basal-membrane localization required for canalization-driven bud outgrowth
Auxin efflux carrier mediating connective auxin transport between buds; repressed by BRC1, induced by CK and nitrogen
Transposase-derived TF; promotes SMXL6/7 transcription AND directly inhibits SPL9 (Motif 3 dual path); degraded under shade
Sugar signaling metabolite; promotes FT and bud outgrowth; reflects sucrose status
Florigen mobile signal; promotes flowering and modulates branching by repressing BRC1
HD-Zip I TF (representing HB21/40/53 redundant family); activates NCED3 downstream of BRC1
Rate-limiting ABA biosynthesis enzyme; activated by HB21 and shade
Stress and dormancy hormone; inhibits bud outgrowth as second messenger downstream of BRC1
Number of axillary branches; integrative phenotype output

edges.mechanism: Removes major source of auxin in the stem

evidence.title: Roles for Auxin, Cytokinin, and Strigolactone in Regulating Shoot Branching
evidence.evidence_sentence: Reducing the stem IAA content via decapitation results in a strong decrease in their expression

edges.mechanism: Auxin upregulates MAX3/CCD7 expression in AXR1-dependent manner

evidence.title: Interactions between Auxin and Strigolactone in Shoot Branching Control
evidence.evidence_sentence: Auxin up-regulation of MAX3 and MAX4 transcripts in an AXR1-dependent manner
evidence.claim: Auxin promotes SL biosynthesis via CCD7

edges.mechanism: Auxin upregulates MAX4/CCD8 expression in AXR1-dependent manner

evidence.title: Interactions between Auxin and Strigolactone in Shoot Branching Control
evidence.evidence_sentence: MAX3 and MAX4 transcripts are induced by auxin in an AXR1-dependent manner
evidence.claim: Auxin promotes SL biosynthesis via CCD8

edges.mechanism: CCD7/MAX3 catalyzes carotenoid cleavage in SL biosynthesis

evidence.title: Interactions between Auxin and Strigolactone in Shoot Branching Control
evidence.evidence_sentence: MAX3 and MAX4 encode plastid-targeted carotenoid cleavage dioxygenases

edges.mechanism: CCD8/MAX4 catalyzes carotenoid cleavage in SL biosynthesis

evidence.title: Interactions between Auxin and Strigolactone in Shoot Branching Control
evidence.evidence_sentence: MAX4 encodes plastid-targeted carotenoid cleavage dioxygenase

edges.mechanism: D27 is an isomerase required for SL biosynthesis

evidence.title: The molecular and genetic regulation of shoot branching
evidence.evidence_sentence: Strigolactone synthesis mutants including D27 exhibit increased branching
evidence.claim: D27 is required for SL biosynthesis

edges.mechanism: Cytochrome P450 oxidizes carlactone to carlactonoic acid

evidence.title: LATERAL BRANCHING OXIDOREDUCTASE Acts in the Final Stages of Strigolactone Biosynthesis in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: In Arabidopsis, CL is oxidized by the MAX1 cytochrome P450 to CLA
evidence.claim: MAX1 converts carlactone to carlactonoic acid

edges.mechanism: 2-OGD oxidoreductase; final known enzymatic step

evidence.title: LATERAL BRANCHING OXIDOREDUCTASE Acts in the Final Stages of Strigolactone Biosynthesis in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: LBO protein specifically converts MeCLA to an unidentified strigolactone-like compound
evidence.claim: LBO acts in the final stages of SL biosynthesis

edges.mechanism: Decapitation causes up to 10,000-fold reduction in RMS1/CCD8 expression

evidence.title: Roles for Auxin, Cytokinin, and Strigolactone in Regulating Shoot Branching
evidence.evidence_sentence: RMS1 expression was reduced as much as 10,000-fold

edges.mechanism: Sugar availability suppresses the auxin-induced SL pathway

evidence.title: Sugar availability suppresses the auxin-induced strigolactone pathway to promote bud outgrowth
evidence.evidence_sentence: Sucrose antagonises auxin effect on bud outgrowth dose-dependently, represses bud response to strigolactones

edges.mechanism: Low nitrogen increases SL biosynthetic gene expression; high nitrogen reduces SL

evidence.title: Auxin and Strigolactone Required for Branching by Nitrogen
evidence.evidence_sentence: Low N results in increased auxin export from active shoot apices

edges.mechanism: SL binds D14 receptor causing conformational change

evidence.title: Strigolactone perception and deactivation by a hydrolase receptor DWARF14
evidence.evidence_sentence: Intact SL molecules induce the active signaling state of D14

edges.mechanism: D14 hydrolyzes SL and undergoes proteasome-mediated self-degradation after perception (Motif 6 self-limiting feedback)

evidence.title: Strigolactone perception and deactivation by a hydrolase receptor DWARF14
evidence.evidence_sentence: D14 deactivates bioactive SLs by hydrolytic degradation

edges.mechanism: Activated D14 directly targets SMXL6/7/8 for ubiquitination via the D14-MAX2 SCF complex (Perception Gate co-inhibitor 1)

evidence.title: SL targets D53-Like SMXL Repressor Proteins
evidence.evidence_sentence: D14-SMXL6 interaction was enhanced upon GR24 treatment

evidence.title: SMAX1-LIKE/D53 Family Members Enable Distinct MAX2-Dependent Responses
evidence.evidence_sentence: SMXL7-YFP was not degraded after rac-GR24 treatment in d14 backgrounds

edges.mechanism: Constitutive MAX2 F-box scaffolds the SCF E3 ligase that ubiquitinates SMXL6/7/8 (Perception Gate co-inhibitor 2)

evidence.title: Strigolactone Signaling in Arabidopsis Regulates Shoot Development by Targeting D53-Like SMXL Repressor Proteins
evidence.evidence_sentence: GR24 causes ubiquitination and degradation of SMXL6, 7, and 8; this requires D14 and MAX2

evidence.title: SMAX1-LIKE/D53 Family Members Enable Distinct MAX2-Dependent Responses to Strigolactones and Karrikins
evidence.evidence_sentence: SMXL7-YFP was not degraded after rac-GR24 treatment in max2 backgrounds
evidence.claim: MAX2 is required for SMXL7 degradation

edges.mechanism: MAX2-SCF independently ubiquitinates BES1 protein (Multi-Output Scaffold, Motif 5)

evidence.title: BES1 Functions as the Co-regulator of D53-like SMXLs to Inhibit BRC1 Expression
evidence.evidence_sentence: Both phosphorylated and dephosphorylated BES1s are induced to be degraded by MAX2

edges.mechanism: SMXL6/7/8 act as transcriptional co-repressors of BRC1 via TPR2 recruitment

evidence.title: Strigolactone Signaling targets D53-Like SMXL Repressor Proteins
evidence.evidence_sentence: BRC1 gene was induced in smxl6/7/8 triple mutant

evidence.title: FHY3 and FAR1 integrate light and strigolactone signaling
evidence.evidence_sentence: Addition of SMXL6 protein significantly reduced activation activity of SPL9 on BRC1 promoter

evidence.title: BES1 Functions as the Co-regulator of D53-like SMXLs to Inhibit BRC1 Expression
evidence.evidence_sentence: SMXL7 and BES1 corporately inhibited the expression of BRC1:LUC

edges.mechanism: SMXL6/7/8 physically interact with SPL9 to inhibit its DNA-binding and transactivation activity

evidence.title: FHY3 and FAR1 integrate light and strigolactone signaling to regulate branching
evidence.evidence_sentence: Addition of SMXL6 protein significantly reduced activation activity of SPL9

edges.mechanism: SMXL6/7/8 promote PIN1 accumulation at the basal plasma membrane

evidence.title: Strigolactone regulates shoot development through a core signalling pathway
evidence.evidence_sentence: Loss of smxl6, smxl7 and smxl8 causes dramatic reduction in PIN1

evidence.title: SMAX1-LIKE/D53 Family Members Enable Distinct MAX2-Dependent Responses
evidence.evidence_sentence: Reduced PIN1-GFP accumulation at basal membrane in smxl6,7,8 max2

edges.mechanism: SL induces CKX9 transcription to promote CK degradation. NOTE (Trap 5 framework limitation, iter 2): this is a non-gated direct edge from Strigolactone since no curated SMXL678->CKX9 edge exists. Signaling-mutant rescue tests (max2+GR24, d14+GR24) for CKX9-mediated CK changes may mispredict because exogenous SL bypasses receptor KO via this direct edge. Acceptable per BUILDER_AGENT.md TRAP 5: flag, dont distort.

evidence.title: Biological Functions of Strigolactones and Their Crosstalk With Other Phytohormones
evidence.evidence_sentence: SLs activate CK catabolism via cytokinin oxidase

edges.mechanism: Cytokinin oxidase/dehydrogenase degrades cytokinins

evidence.title: Biological Functions of Strigolactones and Their Crosstalk With Other Phytohormones
evidence.evidence_sentence: Activate CK catabolism through CKX

edges.mechanism: GA promotes DELLA protein degradation via ubiquitin-proteasome pathway

evidence.title: Gibberellin repression of axillary bud formation in Arabidopsis by modulation of DELLA-SPL9 complex activity
evidence.evidence_sentence: High levels of GAs activate the APC/C complex and promote degradation

edges.mechanism: DELLA proteins sequester SPL9, preventing its activation of BRC1

evidence.title: Gibberellin repression of axillary bud formation in Arabidopsis by modulation of DELLA-SPL9 complex activity
evidence.evidence_sentence: GA-DELLA-SPL9 pathway in axillary bud regulation

edges.mechanism: miR156 post-transcriptionally silences SPL9 mRNA

evidence.title: The microRNA regulated SBP-box genes SPL9 and SPL15 control shoot maturation
evidence.evidence_sentence: SPL9 and SPL15 are post-transcriptionally regulated by miR156

edges.mechanism: Shade (EOD-FR) triggers rapid FHY3 protein degradation via proteasome

evidence.title: FHY3 and FAR1 integrate light and strigolactone signaling
evidence.evidence_sentence: Accumulation of FHY3 protein rapidly decreased after EOD-FR treatment

edges.mechanism: FHY3 directly binds SMXL6 first exon and SMXL7 promoter to activate transcription (Motif 3 path 1)

evidence.title: FHY3 and FAR1 integrate light and strigolactone signaling
evidence.evidence_sentence: FHY3 could directly bind to first exon of SMXL6 and SMXL7; transcript levels upregulated in FHY3-OE

edges.mechanism: FHY3 physically interacts with SPL9 to inhibit DNA-binding (Motif 3 path 2 - both paths reduce BRC1 activation)

evidence.title: FHY3 and FAR1 integrate light and strigolactone signaling
evidence.evidence_sentence: FHY3 can inhibit the DNA-binding activity of both SPL9 and SPL15

edges.mechanism: Shade (low R:FR) upregulates BRC1 via phyB inactivation

evidence.title: BRANCHED1 Promotes Axillary Bud Dormancy in Response to Shade
evidence.evidence_sentence: After 4 h, BRC1 mRNA levels were higher in W+FR-treated plants

edges.mechanism: SPL9 directly binds BRC1 promoter to activate transcription

evidence.title: FHY3 and FAR1 integrate light and strigolactone signaling to regulate branching
evidence.evidence_sentence: SPL9 and SPL15 could directly bind to the S1 fragment of BRC1 promoter

edges.mechanism: Auxin indirectly maintains BRC1 expression via SL promotion and CK suppression

evidence.title: Arabidopsis BRANCHED1 Acts as an Integrator of Branching Signals
evidence.evidence_sentence: BRC1 activity is necessary for the auxin-induced control of apical dominance

edges.mechanism: CK rapidly downregulates BRC1 expression in axillary buds

evidence.title: BRC1 expression regulates bud activation potential
evidence.evidence_sentence: BRC1 transcripts decreased in buds within 1 h of basal CK treatment

edges.mechanism: BES1 directly binds BRC1 promoter and cooperates with SMXLs to repress BRC1

evidence.title: BES1 Functions as the Co-regulator of D53-like SMXLs to Inhibit BRC1 Expression
evidence.evidence_sentence: BES1 directly bound to the BRC1 promoter fragments F2, F4, and F5

edges.mechanism: FT and BRC1 have reciprocal repressive interaction

evidence.title: Plasticity of bud outgrowth varies at cauline and rosette nodes

edges.mechanism: Sugar/T6P signaling suppresses BRC1 expression

evidence.title: Abscisic acid signaling is controlled by a BRANCHED1/HD-ZIP I cascade

edges.mechanism: TIE1 binds BRC1 protein and recruits TPL/TPR co-repressors to inhibit BRC1 activity

evidence.title: TIE1 transcriptional repressor controls shoot branching by directly repressing BRANCHED1
evidence.evidence_sentence: By binding to BRC1, TIE1 inhibits BRC1 activity and consequently represses the transcription of many BRC1 target genes

edges.mechanism: Decapitation rapidly downregulates BRC1 expression in buds

evidence.title: Arabidopsis BRANCHED1 Acts as an Integrator of Branching Signals
evidence.evidence_sentence: BRC1 was downregulated significantly in decapitated plants at 1 h after decapitation

edges.mechanism: Nitrate upregulates IPT expression promoting CK biosynthesis

evidence.evidence_sentence: Nitrate can up-regulate IPT expression and is suggested to regulate bud outgrowth via CK

evidence.title: Auxin, cytokinin and the control of shoot branching

evidence.evidence_sentence: CsBRC1 directly binds to the CsPIN3 promoter to repress the transcription

evidence.evidence_sentence: Within 15 to 30 min of supplying Suc exogenously, Tre6P began to rise rapidly

evidence.evidence_sentence: HB21 mRNA levels decreased after sugar treatment

evidence.evidence_sentence: HB21, HB40, and HB53 enhance NCED3 expression

evidence.evidence_sentence: NCED3 induction in W+FR light

evidence.evidence_sentence: NCED3 encodes an enzyme that catalyzes xanthoxin production, a key regulatory step of ABA biosynthesis

edges.mechanism: ABA2 catalyzes ABA biosynthetic step

evidence.evidence_sentence: Bud n and bud n-2 of aba2-1 had less than 25% of the ABA as the wild type
evidence.claim: ABA2 is required for ABA biosynthesis

edges.mechanism: PIN1 enables auxin canalization from buds into stem PATS required for bud activation

evidence.evidence_sentence: BRC1 prevents AM initiation and retards all stages of bud development
